criteria.name: controls_are_keyboard_accessible

criteria.name: modals_manage_focus
criteria.description: Export drawer traps focus while open and returns it to the originating control on Escape or close; popovers return focus similarly
criteria.reasoning: The export drawer exposed a modal dialog with aria-modal=true, trapped Tab focus within the dialog, and Escape returned focus to the Open export drawer control. Evidence: modals_manage_focus-shared.png

criteria.name: images_and_icons_have_alt_text

criteria.reasoning: Avatar images had descriptive alt text, and icon-only buttons had accessible aria-label names such as Open navigation, Open notifications, and Close export drawer. Evidence: controls_are_keyboard_accessible-shared.png

criteria.name: feedback_uses_live_regions

criteria.reasoning: Submitting the empty Add User form displayed validation errors and a polite live region containing the complete validation message. Evidence: feedback_uses_live_regions-shared.png

criteria.name: forms_have_explicit_labels

criteria.reasoning: Add User fields used visible label elements explicitly associated with controls by matching for/id values. Evidence: forms_have_explicit_labels-shared.png

criteria.name: actions_use_specific_labels
criteria.description: Where the app renders button or action labels, they are specific verbs (Add user, Change status, Export session, Copy), not generic (Submit, OK) when a specific label is possible.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: generic labels such as “Export,” “Delete,” and “Cancel” are present where more specific labels could be used. Evidence: actions_use_specific_labels-shared.png

criteria.name: errors_name_problem_and_fix
criteria.description: Where the app renders validation or import errors, they name the field and the fix (for example firstName length or email format), not just a rejection (Invalid).
criteria.reasoning: yes — validation messages identify fields and requirements, such as “email is required” and “temporaryPassword must be at least 8 characters.” Evidence: errors_name_problem_and_fix-after.png

criteria.name: empty_states_explain_next_step
criteria.description: Where the app renders empty states, the copy explains what belongs there and how to add it.
criteria.reasoning: yes — the empty state says “Try clearing the search or filters, or add a new user to the directory” and provides an “Add User” action. Evidence: empty_states_explain_next_step-shared.png

criteria.name: body_copy_is_well_written
criteria.description: Where the app renders body or helper copy in Users and Export, rate how free it is of spelling and grammatical errors.
criteria.reasoning: yes — Users and Export copy is grammatical, clear, and free of observed spelling errors. Evidence: body_copy_is_well_written-shared.png

criteria.name: terminology_is_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders labels for the same concept in multiple places, terminology is consistent (User vs Users, Session JSON tab label).
criteria.reasoning: yes — “Users,” “Add User,” “Session JSON,” and related labels are used consistently across views and export UI. Evidence: body_copy_is_well_written-shared.png

criteria.name: numbers_dates_and_units_are_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders KPI numbers, payments totals, and export timestamps, formatting is consistent.
criteria.reasoning: yes — KPIs use compact notation and percentages consistently, payments use dollar formatting with comma separators, and export timestamps are clearly labeled. Evidence: body_copy_is_well_written-shared.png

criteria.name: success_messages_are_specific
criteria.description: Where the app renders confirmation after create, delete, copy, or import, they state what happened rather than vague affirmations.
criteria.reasoning: yes — the confirmation explicitly states “Copied Session JSON” and “Session JSON copied (via MCP).” Evidence: success_messages_are_specific-shared.png
